children.heading: Commandant
children.label: 1 Commandant
children.text: The term “Commandant” means the Commandant of the Coast Guard.

children.heading: Lighthouse
children.label: 2 Lighthouse
children.text: The term “Lighthouse” means the Jupiter Inlet Lighthouse located in Palm Beach County, Florida.

children.label: A
children.text: Palm Beach County, Florida;

children.label: B
children.text: the Town of Jupiter, Florida;

children.label: C
children.text: the Village of Tequesta, Florida; and

children.label: D
children.text: the Loxahatchee River Historical Society.

children.heading: Management plan
children.label: 4 Management plan
children.text: The term “management plan” means the management plan developed under subsection (c)(1).

children.heading: Map
children.label: 5 Map
children.text: The term “map” means the map entitled “Jupiter Inlet Lighthouse Outstanding Natural Area” and dated .October 29, 2007

children.heading: Outstanding Natural Area
children.label: 6 Outstanding Natural Area
children.text: The term “Outstanding Natural Area” means the Jupiter Inlet Lighthouse Outstanding Natural Area established by subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: Public land
children.label: 7 Public land
children.text: The term “public land” has the meaning given the term “public lands” in section 103(e) of the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 ().43 U.S.C. 1702(e)

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 8 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: State
children.label: 9 State
children.text: The term “State” means the State of Florida.

children.heading: Establishment of the Jupiter Inlet Lighthouse Outstanding Natural Area
children.label: b Establishment of the Jupiter Inlet Lighthouse Outstanding Natural Area

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: Subject to valid existing rights, there is established for the purposes described in paragraph (2) the Jupiter Inlet Lighthouse Outstanding Natural Area, the boundaries of which are depicted on the map.

children.label: A
children.text: allowing certain recreational and research activities to continue in the Outstanding Natural Area; and

children.label: B
children.text: ensuring that Coast Guard operations and activities are unimpeded within the boundaries of the Outstanding Natural Area.

children.heading: Availability of map
children.label: 3 Availability of map
children.text: The map shall be on file and available for public inspection in appropriate offices of the Bureau of Land Management.

children.label: i
children.text: all forms of entry, appropriation, or disposal under the public land laws;

children.label: ii
children.text: location, entry, and patent under the mining laws; and

children.label: iii
children.text: operation of the mineral leasing and geothermal leasing laws and the mineral materials laws.

children.label: i
children.text: the Executive Order dated ;October 22, 1854

children.label: ii
children.text: Executive Order No. 4254 (); andJune 12, 1925

children.label: iii
children.text: Public Land Order No. 7202 (61 Fed. Reg. 29758).

children.label: A
children.text: provide long-term management guidance for the public land in the Outstanding Natural Area; and

children.label: B
children.text: ensure that the Outstanding Natural Area fulfills the purposes for which the Outstanding Natural Area is established.

children.label: A
children.text: in consultation with appropriate Federal, State, county, and local government agencies, the Commandant, the Local Partners, and other partners; and

children.label: B
children.text: in a manner that ensures full public participation.

children.heading: Existing plans
children.label: 3 Existing plans
children.text: The management plan shall, to the maximum extent practicable, be consistent with existing resource plans, policies, and programs.

children.label: i
children.text: the protection and conservation of the resource values of the Outstanding Natural Area; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the restoration of native plant communities and estuaries in the Outstanding Natural Area, with an emphasis on the conservation and enhancement of healthy, functioning ecological systems in perpetuity;

children.label: B
children.text: objectives and provisions to maintain or recreate historic structures;

children.label: C
children.text: an implementation plan for a program of interpretation and public education about the natural and cultural resources of the Lighthouse, the public land surrounding the Lighthouse, and associated structures;

children.label: i
children.text: are compatible with achieving the resource objectives for the Outstanding Natural Area described in subsection (d)(1)(A)(ii); and

children.label: ii
children.text: would accommodate visitors to the Outstanding Natural Area;

children.label: E
children.text: natural and cultural resource management strategies for the Outstanding Natural Area, to be developed in consultation with appropriate departments of the State, the Local Partners, and the Commandant, with an emphasis on resource conservation in the Outstanding Natural Area and the interpretive, educational, and long-term scientific uses of the resources; and

children.label: F
children.text: recreational use strategies for the Outstanding Natural Area, to be prepared in consultation with the Local Partners, appropriate departments of the State, and the Coast Guard, with an emphasis on passive recreation.

children.heading: Interim plan
children.label: 5 Interim plan
children.text: Until a management plan is adopted for the Outstanding Natural Area, the Jupiter Inlet Coordinated Resource Management Plan (including any updates or amendments to the Jupiter Inlet Coordinated Resource Management Plan) shall be in effect.

children.heading: Management of the Jupiter Inlet Lighthouse Outstanding Natural Area
children.label: d Management of the Jupiter Inlet Lighthouse Outstanding Natural Area
i
children.text: as part of the National Landscape Conservation System;

children.label: ii
children.text: in a manner that conserves, protects, and enhances the unique and nationally important historical, natural, cultural, scientific, educational, scenic, and recreational values of the Outstanding Natural Area, including an emphasis on the restoration of native ecological systems; and

children.label: iii
children.text: in accordance with the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 ( et seq.) and other applicable laws.43 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: In managing the Outstanding Natural Area, the Secretary shall not take any action that precludes, prohibits, or otherwise affects the conduct of ongoing or future Coast Guard operations or activities on lots 16 and 18, as depicted on the map.

children.heading: Uses
children.label: 2 Uses
children.text: Subject to valid existing rights and subsection (e), the Secretary shall only allow uses of the Outstanding Natural Area that the Secretary, in consultation with the Commandant and Local Partners, determines would likely further the purposes for which the Outstanding Natural Area is established.

children.heading: Cooperative agreements
children.label: 3 Cooperative agreements
children.text: To facilitate implementation of the management plan and to continue the successful partnerships with local communities and other partners, the Secretary may, in accordance with section 307(b) of the Federal Land Management Policy and Management Act of 1976 (), enter into cooperative agreements with the appropriate Federal, State, county, other local government agencies, and other partners (including the Loxahatchee River Historical Society) for the long-term management of the Outstanding Natural Area.143 U.S.C. 1737(b)So in original. The word “Management” probably should not appear.1

children.heading: Research activities
children.label: 4 Research activities
children.text: To continue successful research partnerships, pursue future research partnerships, and assist in the development and implementation of the management plan, the Secretary may, in accordance with section 307(a) of the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 (), authorize the conduct of appropriate research activities in the Outstanding Natural Area for the purposes described in subsection (b)(2).43 U.S.C. 1737(a)

children.label: i
children.text: adjacent to the Outstanding Natural Area; and

children.label: ii
children.text: identified in the management plan as appropriate for acquisition.

children.heading: Means of acquisition
children.label: B Means of acquisition
children.text: Land or an interest in land may be acquired under subparagraph (A) only by donation, exchange, or purchase from a willing seller with donated or appropriated funds.

children.heading: Additions to the Outstanding Natural Area
children.label: C Additions to the Outstanding Natural Area
children.text: Any land or interest in land adjacent to the Outstanding Natural Area acquired by the United States after , under subparagraph (A) shall be added to, and administered as part of, the Outstanding Natural Area.May 8, 2008

children.label: A
children.text: any maritime security, maritime safety, or environmental protection mission or activity of the Coast Guard;

children.label: B
children.text: any border security operation or law enforcement activity by the Department of Homeland Security or the Department of Justice; or

children.label: C
children.text: any law enforcement activity of any Federal, State, or local law enforcement agency in the Outstanding Natural Area.

children.heading: Future disposition of Coast Guard facilities
children.label: 7 Future disposition of Coast Guard facilities
children.text: If the Commandant determines, after , that Coast Guard facilities within the Outstanding Natural Area exceed the needs of the Coast Guard, the Commandant may relinquish the facilities to the Secretary without removal, subject only to any environmental remediation that may be required by law.May 8, 2008

children.heading: Effect on ongoing and future Coast Guard operations
children.label: e Effect on ongoing and future Coast Guard operations

children.text: the continued and future operation of, access to, maintenance of, and, as may be necessitated for Coast Guard missions, the expansion, enhancement, or replacement of, the Coast Guard High Frequency antenna site on lot 16;

the continued and future operation of, access to, maintenance of, and, as may be necessitated for Coast Guard missions, the expansion, enhancement, or replacement of, the military family housing area on lot 18;
the continued and future use of, access to, maintenance of, and, as may be necessitated for Coast Guard missions, the expansion, enhancement, or replacement of, the pier on lot 18;
the existing lease of the Jupiter Inlet Lighthouse on lot 18 from the Coast Guard to the Loxahatchee River Historical Society; or
any easements or other less-than-fee interests in property appurtenant to existing Coast Guard facilities on lots 16 and 18.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: f Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out this section.
